Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Awesome. I've been wanting to have this for ages. Thanks for finally implementing it. 

One improvement that would make this much more useful would be to change the .spec file and Debian rules so that this is actually installed. Otherwise, it will exist but few people will actually know it exists and use it.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Passed enforced test review-ldiskfs-dne on CentOS 7.0/x86_64 uploaded by Onyx Autotest from onyx-33vm6: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/da3d1954-d306-471d-b48b-c9202a53b454 ran 4 tests.

Patch Set 1:

> One improvement that would make this much more useful would be to
 > change the .spec file and Debian rules so that this is actually
 > installed. Otherwise, it will exist but few people will actually
 > know it exists and use it.

I think it'd make more sense to install from the actual build system rather than each distro's package management files (well, both, as the spec needs to reflect on newly installed files); but honestly got a bit lost with the automake files.
I think I've found something usable here https://www.swansontec.com/bash-completion.html and will monkey something into lustre-build.m4 in a v2 here today
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 2:

tested on el7:
 $ rpm -qpl lustre-2.12.0_RC3_868_g0e95b51_dirty-1.el7.centos.x86_64.rpm | grep bash-compl
 /usr/share/bash-completion/completions/lctl

Hopefully the clientdistro parameter for tests will test deb packages at least build; I'm not too sure about adding it to both lustre-client-utils and lustre-server-utils but if we add lfs eventually I'd be hard pressed to pick one... And there already are wildcards there so I'm not sure how it works. But the hard part was the autoconf stuff anyway, that should be somewhat ok

Patch Set 1:

> I think it'd make more sense to install from the actual build
 > system rather than each distro's package management files (well,
 > both, as the spec needs to reflect on newly installed files);

We need it to be part of the packages, since many users do not build their own binaries.

 > honestly got a bit lost with the automake files.
 > I think I've found something usable here https://www.swansontec.com/bash-completion.html
 > and will monkey something into lustre-build.m4 in a v2 here today

I don't think you really need to do anything with autoconf, since we want to (IMHO) always install the completion file.  The only thing that is needed is the update lustre/scripts/Makefile.am (I don't think we need the "completions" subdirectory) to install lctl.bash like:

 completiondir = /usr/share/bash-completion/completions
 completion_SCRIPTS = lctl.bash

and add it to lustre.spec.in for the utils package:

 %files -f lustre.files
 :
 :
 %{_datadir}/bash-completion/completions/*

Not totally sure about how to do it with Debian, but once it is in RHEL then someone will also add it there.

Patch Set 2:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 2:

Builds for x86_64 centos7,rhel8.0 successful
Job output URL: http://testing.linuxhacker.ru:3333/lustre-reports/2746/results.html

 Commencing initial testing: runtests@ldiskfs+DNE runtests-ssk@ldiskfs+SharedKey runtests@zfs

Patch Set 2:

> > I think it'd make more sense to install from the actual build
 > > system rather than each distro's package management files (well,
 > > both, as the spec needs to reflect on newly installed files);
 > 
 > We need it to be part of the packages, since many users do not
 > build their own binaries.

Yes, definitely - I meant that as an "as well"; I've just seen too many spec files that copy the file directly from the sources to not both with automake/conf and that's what I didn't want to do.

 > > honestly got a bit lost with the automake files.
 > > I think I've found something usable here https://www.swansontec.com/bash-completion.html
 > > and will monkey something into lustre-build.m4 in a v2 here today
 > 
 > I don't think you really need to do anything with autoconf, since
 > we want to (IMHO) always install the completion file.  The only
 > thing that is needed is the update lustre/scripts/Makefile.am (I
 > don't think we need the "completions" subdirectory) to install
 > lctl.bash like:

Hmm, technically could be another directory I guess, since the source I used wanted to query pkg-config for the location.

Happy to revert this bit and hardcode the path if you don't feel that's necessary.

 > completiondir = /usr/share/bash-completion/completions
 > completion_SCRIPTS = lctl.bash

(I renamed the file to 'lctl' in a bash-specific dir instead to have clean install names, I didn't see an easy/clean way of installing something with a different name than the source file)

 > Not totally sure about how to do it with Debian, but once it is in
 > RHEL then someone will also add it there.

If test fails I'll just remove the lines I added in the debian files, that was just a guess more than anything else

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2

(1 comment)

I didn't notice the new patch before I posted my previous comments.

Patch looks good.  Minor cleanup if it is refreshed for some other reason.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

I removed the extra "Test-Parameters: trivial" line, since that didn't really add any benefit.  There will always be a review-ldiskfs test run, even if "trivial" is used.  The "trivial" keyword affects the whole patch, so no need to list it for each Test-Parameters run, if there are multiple different test configs to be run.

Note that there may be a problem with provisioning the Ubuntu1804 client at this time, so if we are still having problems I'd suggest to remove that extra test case.

Patch Set 4:

> I removed the extra "Test-Parameters: trivial" line, since that
 > didn't really add any benefit.  There will always be a
 > review-ldiskfs test run, even if "trivial" is used.  The "trivial"
 > keyword affects the whole patch, so no need to list it for each
 > Test-Parameters run, if there are multiple different test configs
 > to be run.
 > 
 > Note that there may be a problem with provisioning the Ubuntu1804
 > client at this time, so if we are still having problems I'd suggest
 > to remove that extra test case.

Thanks, it looks like it works sometimes so the packaging itself that I wanted to test is probably ok... Not quite sure what to do with the current score though, it looks like we kept the -1 from patchset 3 as gerrit knows a commit message update doesn't reset the score, but we didn't get the +1 that was set later on (obsolete) patchset 3, and the new ones didn't change the score whether fail or pass.
I think that'd need some trivial change in a file to reset the score more than removing the clientdistro test param?

Letting you handle that when able.

parents.subject: LU-12707 obdecho: avoid panic with partially object init

commit.subject: LU-12734 misc: add bash completion for lctl set/get_param
commit.message:
LU-12734 misc: add bash completion for lctl set/get_param

Add some start of bash completion for lctl, mainly set_param and
get_param, and modify build system to install it.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Dominique Martinet <dominique.martinet@cea.fr>
Change-Id: I16d2698e782702375c7fa3edf3bfde2e3b197297
